plain_definition: Japan's integrated financial regulator -- broadly the equivalent of Hong Kong's SFC and HKMA combined -- responsible for registering and supervising banks, securities firms, insurers, crypto-asset exchange service providers, and electronic payment instrument (stablecoin) exchange operators. The FSA has no separate crypto-asset statute of its own; instead it administers the crypto-asset and stablecoin provisions Japan has added, over a series of amendments since 2016, to the existing Payment Services Act and Financial Instruments and Exchange Act, and recognises the self-regulatory rulebooks of bodies such as JVCEA that sit on top of its registration regime.
formal_definition: The Financial Services Agency (金融庁) is Japan's integrated financial regulator. It is the registration and supervisory authority for crypto-asset exchange service providers under Payment Services Act (PSA) Art. 63-2, for electronic payment instrument exchange business operators under PSA Art. 62-3, and, from 1 June 2026, for electronic payment instrument / crypto-asset service intermediary business operators under PSA Art. 63-22-2 (registration is formally by the Prime Minister, administered in practice by the FSA); it also supervises crypto-asset exchange service providers and electronic payment instrument service providers as 'specified business operators' under the Act on Prevention of Transfer of Criminal Proceeds. The FSA maintains official English- and Japanese-language RSS news feeds (fsa.go.jp/fsaEnNewsList_rss2.xml and fsa.go.jp/fsaNewsListAll_rss2.xml) and publishes the current lists of associations it has certified under the PSA and FIEA, including JVCEA -- all confirmed by direct fetch this session.
